expectations.text: Output begins with the literal line `Findings:`
All eight preamble fields are present in order
expectations.evidence: Lines 2-9 contain all eight required fields in the specified order

expectations.text: Findings are tagged with severity in backticks (`High`, `Medium`, `Low`, or `Critical` if a higher tier is used)
expectations.evidence: Every finding bullet starts with a backtick severity: `High`, `Medium`, or `Low`

expectations.text: The use of POST sub-resource verb (`/api/tasks/:id/complete`) is identified as inconsistent with REST conventions — should be PATCH on the resource
expectations.evidence: First High finding: 'State transition is modeled as `POST /api/tasks/:id/complete` — a sub-resource action verb instead of a `PATCH` on the task resource. ... Replace with `PATCH /api/tasks/:id`'

expectations.text: The missing Idempotency-Key on the side-effectful POST is identified as a finding
expectations.evidence: Second High finding: 'No `Idempotency-Key` handling on a side-effectful, non-GET request that updates state, sends an email, and writes an audit row'

expectations.text: The snake_case wire field `task_id` is identified as a finding (should be camelCase)
expectations.evidence: First Medium finding: 'Response uses `task_id` (snake_case) on the wire ... Return `{ "taskId": ..., "status": "COMPLETED" }`'

expectations.text: The `200` status (rather than reflecting the state-transition correctly) and the bypass of the canonical error envelope are noted where relevant
expectations.evidence: Medium finding addresses 200 status semantics; another Medium finding addresses missing error handling that bypasses the canonical `{ code, message, details?, requestId }` envelope

expectations.text: Findings are ordered by severity, highest first
Output does not cite either skill by name (no mention of 'structured-code-review' or 'rest-api-design' as the source of any rule)
expectations.evidence: Neither 'structured-code-review' nor 'rest-api-design' appears in the output
